questions.title: What's most important to you?
questions.subtitle: Choose up to 2 answers that matter most.

questions.title: What kind of water heater do you currently have?
questions.subtitle: This helps us understand your starting point.

options.label: Building a new home and want to pick

questions.title: What tank size do you have?
questions.subtitle: No problem if you are not sure.

questions.title: What size is your hybrid water heater?
questions.subtitle: No problem if you are not sure.

questions.title: How many people are in the home?
questions.subtitle: This helps estimate hot water demand.

questions.title: How many water heaters do you have?
questions.subtitle: Count separate water heater units.

questions.title: Where is it located?
questions.subtitle: Access and ventilation can affect the best fit.

questions.title: What fuel source does your home use?
questions.subtitle: If your heater has a yellow flexible line, it is usually natural gas.
questions.help: If your heater has a yellow flexible line, it is usually natural gas.

questions.title: Note on electric and tankless
questions.subtitle: Electric-only homes are usually better served by keeping a tank setup, using a heat pump, or adding a second tank.
questions.help: If your home is electric-only, we recommend keeping your setup or adding a second tank in parallel rather than switching to tankless.

questions.title: How many bathrooms does your home have?
questions.subtitle: This helps estimate peak hot water demand.

questions.title: Is your home 1 or 2 stories?
questions.subtitle: Home layout can affect installation requirements.

questions.title: Where should we send your result?
questions.subtitle: Enter your name, email, and ZIP code to see your recommendation.

questions.title: Want help choosing the right water heater?
questions.subtitle: You're in our service area. Share your phone and service address so we can follow up.

results.title: Gas Tankless
results.message: Best for households with high demand, multiple bathrooms, or large families. Offers endless hot water with proper gas supply and venting.
results.tie_breaker_reason: Your answers are balanced, so we recommend gas tankless when demand, bathrooms, or endless hot water are major factors.

in_area.message: Mother serves your ZIP code and can help confirm whether gas tankless is the right fit.

out_of_area.message: Mother is not serving your ZIP code yet, so we cannot book this through our team right now.
out_of_area.advice: Your result is still useful. If you call a local plumber, ask them to confirm gas supply, venting, and installation requirements before choosing tankless.

results.title: Gas Tank
results.message: A great low-cost upgrade if you already have gas service. Reliable and simple installation for small to medium households.
results.tie_breaker_reason: Your answers are balanced, so we recommend a gas tank when lower upfront cost and simple replacement matter most.

in_area.message: Mother serves your ZIP code and can help confirm the right gas tank size.

out_of_area.message: Mother is not serving your ZIP code yet, so we cannot book this through our team right now.
out_of_area.advice: Your result is still useful. If you call a local plumber, ask whether a standard gas tank replacement is the simplest fit for your current setup.

results.title: Electric Tank
results.message: A straightforward option for electric-only homes. Consider adding a second tank to increase hot-water capacity.
results.tie_breaker_reason: Your answers are balanced, so we recommend an electric tank when the home appears electric-only and simplicity matters.

in_area.message: Mother serves your ZIP code and can help confirm the right electric tank setup.

out_of_area.message: Mother is not serving your ZIP code yet, so we cannot book this through our team right now.
out_of_area.advice: Your result is still useful. If you call a local plumber, ask whether your electrical capacity supports the replacement size you want.

results.title: Heat Pump (Hybrid)
results.message: High-efficiency choice for electric homes with space for airflow. Delivers long-term savings with lower energy use.
results.tie_breaker_reason: Your answers are balanced, so we recommend a heat pump when efficiency and long-term utility savings matter most.

in_area.message: Mother serves your ZIP code and can help confirm whether your space works for a heat pump water heater.

out_of_area.message: Mother is not serving your ZIP code yet, so we cannot book this through our team right now.
out_of_area.advice: Your result is still useful. If you call a local plumber, ask about airflow, room size, condensate routing, and noise before choosing a heat pump water heater.

results.title: Add a Second Tank (Parallel)
results.message: Adding a second tank boosts peak capacity without needing extra power or gas lines - perfect for large families.
results.tie_breaker_reason: Your answers are balanced, so we recommend adding a second tank when capacity is the concern but a full conversion may not be necessary.

in_area.message: Mother serves your ZIP code and can help confirm whether a second tank makes sense for your home.

out_of_area.message: Mother is not serving your ZIP code yet, so we cannot book this through our team right now.
out_of_area.advice: Your result is still useful. If you call a local plumber, ask whether adding a second tank in parallel is simpler than changing fuel source or going tankless.
